fact: In Antarctica, this date marks the middle of autumn, when penguins start growing their fluffiest winter feathers that make them look like waddling marshmallows.
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around April 20 in most places - it's like the Earth remembered how to use a compass!
Dandelions across North America hit their peak blooming power right around this date, turning lawns into seas of tiny yellow suns.
April 20 falls smack in the middle of Taurus season, when the zodiac's most stubborn bull is in charge of everyone's personality.
Tree sap flows fastest in maple trees around this time in cold regions - it's like the trees are chugging energy drinks to wake up from winter!
The Northern Hemisphere gains about 3 minutes of daylight every single day around April 20 - that's enough time to brush your teeth with extra sparkle!
Earthworms throw their biggest underground parties around this date because the soil temperature hits the perfect worm-comfort zone of 55°F.
April 20 sits exactly 110 days into the year - making it the mathematical sweet spot between New Year's Day and summer vacation!
Migrating butterflies like monarchs use this date as their natural GPS checkpoint, knowing they're right on schedule for their thousand-mile journeys.
